questions.text: Record a 5-minute video pitch for your capstone project (or the hypothetical dApp you defined in 18.3) as if presenting to the MST scaleGrant committee. Follow the exact pitch structure taught in this sub-module. Upload the video to YouTube (unlisted) or Loom, and submit the link.
questions.explanation: 📊 Assessment Rubric - How Your Pitch Will Be Graded
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Assessment Rubric - How Your Pitch Will Be Graded
Criteria What Earns Full Marks Marks
1. Problem Clarity & Hook Shocking, specific stat within 20 sec. Stakeholder named. Cost quantified. Reviewer instantly understands the pain. 5
2. Solution Demo Live or pre-recorded demo of actual testnet dApp. Transaction shown on MST Explorer. At least one technical insight shared. Clear MST feature usage. 8
3. Traction & Market Evidence Credible evidence: letter of intent, pilot data, beta sign-ups, on-chain metrics. Market size stated with source. Competition addressed. 5
4. Team & Ask Team introduced with relevant experience in ≤2 sentences. Grant track explicitly named. Amount justified. Solo founder addresses sustainability. 5
5. Delivery & Production Quality Within 5:00 ± 15 sec. Eye contact with camera. Clear audio. Smooth demo transition. Calm handling of any glitches. Slides minimal. 4
6. Overall Persuasiveness Pitch leaves the reviewer feeling confident in the builder and excited about the project's potential on MST. Memorable closing. 3
Examiner note: If the demo is entirely missing or consists only of slides with no on-chain evidence, criteria 2 scores 0. If the grant track is not named, criteria 4 loses 2 marks. The pitch is evaluated as if the reviewer has not read your written proposal - it must stand alone.
